title: Review Agent Structured Output (gate verdict)
description:
The structured object a review/verify agent (or a deterministic gate serializer) is FORCED to emit at gate time (spec §2 'capture must be a byproduct, never a step'). A decision ledger row is a direct projection of this object: `ledger.py project` reads a document validating against THIS schema, adds the context fields the reviewer can't know (unit_id, stage, lane, ledger_id, schema_version, round, timestamp) from flags, derives the findings roll-up from the findings array, and emits a row validating against ledger.schema.json. Every decision-time ledger field maps 1:1 onto a field here (or is a pure function of one), so capture never re-types anything.

PROJECTION MAP (review-output field -> ledger field):
  verdict            -> verdict
  verdict_reason     -> verdict_reason
  findings[]         -> finding_ids (each .id), findings_count (len), findings_by_severity (histogram of .severity)
  evidence_claimed   -> evidence_claimed
  evidence_verified  -> evidence_verified
  reviewer_role      -> reviewer_role
  reviewer_model     -> reviewer_model
  reviewer_effort    -> reviewer_effort
  cost_tokens_in     -> cost_tokens_in
  cost_tokens_out    -> cost_tokens_out
  cost_usd           -> cost_usd
  wall_clock_seconds -> wall_clock_seconds
  gate_results       -> (not projected onto a scalar ledger field; informs evidence_verified and verdict; retained for audit)
  merge_recommendation -> (advisory; feeds the merge-stage decision, not a decision-row field)
Context fields supplied by the capture adapter / project flags (NOT emitted by the reviewer):
  ledger_id, schema_version, unit_id, unit_kind, lane, stage, round, timestamp.

evidence_verified.description: Null if this stage did not independently re-verify the claim.

findings.description: One entry per finding. The ledger roll-up (finding_ids / findings_count / findings_by_severity) is derived from this array — the reviewer never hand-counts.

gate_results.description: Per-check results for deterministic/automated gates run as part of this review (compile, tests, lints, schema conformance).
